title.es: Visibilizar la contribución de una persona más callada

question.en: Tell me about a decision where a quieter person had information the group needed. How did you surface their contribution without making it awkward?
question.es: Cuéntame sobre una decisión en la que una persona más callada tenía información que el grupo necesitaba. ¿Cómo hiciste visible su contribución sin que fuera incómodo?

must_include: why the person was less likely to speak — their position in the room, their seniority relative to others, their communication style, or the meeting dynamic that was suppressing their voice
how the candidate knew or suspected the person had relevant information — what they noticed that signaled something was being held back
the specific action the candidate took to surface the contribution — and critically, whether it was done in the room in the moment or set up in advance
how the candidate handled the person's response — including if the person was initially reluctant, and what the candidate did to make space without creating more pressure
what happened to the decision because of the surfaced contribution — whether the information changed anything, and whether the quieter person received visible acknowledgment for it
must_avoid: framing the quieter person as a passive beneficiary of the candidate's advocacy — RFI stories should show the person as an active contributor whose voice the candidate created conditions for, not someone who needed to be rescued
generic 'I made sure everyone had a chance to speak' framing without a specific person, moment, or piece of information
omitting the awkwardness — if the candidate says it wasn't awkward at all, probe whether they actually created real space or just offered a polite opening that could easily be declined
taking credit for the contribution itself: 'I raised their idea and the team went with it' — the credit belongs to the quieter person, not to the candidate who passed it along
